title: Spinal Cord Stimulation for the Failed Back Syndrome
study_question: Is spinal cord stimulation effective for carefully selected patients with failed back syndrome?
population_assessed: 32 patients with failed back syndrome, lower extremity and lumbar pain, post-laminectomy.
follow_up_duration: Up to 5 years (23 patients at 1 year, 8 at 5 years).
outcome_measures: Pain relief, patient employment, narcotic use, recommendation, complications.
inclusion_criteria: Failed back syndrome, lower extremity pain, at least one lumbar laminectomy, no mechanical instability.
answer_to_study_question: SCS provided 50% or better pain relief for 76% at 1 year and 74% at 2 years.
